title: akebia trifoliata subsp. australis
intro: Akebia trifoliata subsp. australis, also known as the Chinese chocolate vine or black vegetable vine, is a type of flowering plant in the Lardizabalaceae family, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Akebia trifoliata subsp. australis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory conditions, and it is also sometimes used as a food ingredient in traditional Asian cuisine.
